title: Humboltia caulescens
intro: Humboldtia caulescens, also known as the Indian poison tree or poison tree, is a plant species native to the Western Ghats of India, belonging to the family Fabaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including its potential health benefits of treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Humboldtia caulescens include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which are thought to contribute to its therapeutic properties. The plant is commonly used to prepare herbal remedies such as decoctions, infusions, and powders, often in the form of traditional Ayurvedic medications, which are traditionally used to treat a range of health issues.
taxonomy: Humboltia caulescens is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. It is native to Mexico and belongs to the genus Humboltia.
